summary: Daily expenses tracking website with Indian Rupee amounts, person-wise entries, expense categories, and month/year-wise summaries with daily averages
architectural_notes: All amounts displayed in Indian Rupees (₹)
Daily average calculated as: Total expenses / Number of days in the period
Summaries show both combined totals and per-person breakdowns
People are managed separately and can be reused across expense entries
Expense entry form supports both selecting from existing people and adding new people inline

existing_features.title: Create an expense entry form component with fields for person name, amount in Indian Rupees (₹), description, date picker, and category dropdown (Food, Transport, Bills, Entertainment, Other)
Create a list view component that displays all expense entries with edit and delete functionality, showing person name, amount in ₹, description, date, and category
Create a summary view component that displays month-wise and year-wise expense summaries with total expenses in ₹ and daily averages
Add per-person expense breakdown section within summary views showing individual totals and daily averages for selected month or year

Design a modern, clean, and professional visual theme with a color palette suitable for a financial tracking application, consistent typography, and intuitive layout that emphasizes readability of monetary values
Create a People Management component with a list view showing all people and an add person form with name input field, using the same styling patterns as ExpenseForm and ExpenseList components
Add edit functionality to People Management component allowing inline editing of person names with save and cancel actions
Add delete functionality to People Management component with confirmation dialog before removing a person
Update ExpenseForm component to replace the free-text person name input with a searchable dropdown selector populated from the managed people list, maintaining the current form layout and validation
Add quick-add functionality to ExpenseForm person selector allowing users to create new people directly from the dropdown without leaving the expense entry form

Create a PersonDetailView component that displays a specific person's complete expense history in a table format matching ExpenseList styling, showing all expense details (amount, description, date, category) sorted by date
Add summary statistics section to PersonDetailView showing total spending in ₹ and daily average calculated across all time for that person
Add a People tab to the main App navigation alongside Expenses and Summary tabs, displaying the People Management component when selected
Add clickable links from person names in ExpenseList and PersonBreakdown components that navigate to the PersonDetailView for that specific person

Create React Query hooks in useQueries.ts for people management: useCreatePerson, useListPeople, useUpdatePerson, useDeletePerson, and usePersonExpenses following the existing pattern for expense hooks

new_feature_requests.summary: Expense entry form with person name, amount (₹), description, date, and category
List view of all expense entries with editing and deletion capabilities
Month and year-wise expense summaries showing total expenses and daily averages
Expense categories: Food, Transport, Bills, Entertainment, Other

Create a backend data model for expense entries that stores person name (Text), amount in Indian Rupees (Nat), description (Text), date (Int timestamp), and category (Text variant with options: Food, Transport, Bills, Entertainment, Other)
Implement backend functions to create, read, update, and delete expense entries with stable storage persistence
Implement backend query functions to calculate month-wise and year-wise expense summaries including total expenses and daily average for each period
Implement backend query function to calculate per-person expense breakdowns for any given time period (month or year)
People management interface to add, edit, and remove people
Update expense entry form to select from list of managed people with option to add new people on-the-fly
Individual person detail page showing complete expense history, total spending, and daily averages
Create a backend data model to store and manage people with fields for id (unique identifier), name (Text), and createdAt (Int timestamp for creation date)

Implement backend CRUD functions for people: createPerson(name: Text) returns Person, listPeople() returns all people sorted by name, updatePerson(id, name) updates person name, deletePerson(id) removes person and returns success status

Implement backend query function getPersonExpenses(personId: Text, year: ?Nat, month: ?Nat) that returns all expenses for a specific person, optionally filtered by year and/or month, sorted by date descending
